# -*- sh -*-
# See http://rigaux.org/language-study/diagram.html for the format of this file
#claude: auto-generated by Claude Code (inspired from my other lineage.txt)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# UNIX utilities lineage
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

#src: wikipedia articles on different programs
#src: UNIX man pages (V1-V7, BSD, Plan 9)

# For shells (sh, csh, bash) see shells/lineage.txt
# For awk/sed/Perl see shells/lineage.txt (non shell section)
# For networking tools (curl, wget, etc.) see networking/lineage.txt

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pre-Historical (Multics/CTSS)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Multics had rudimentary file utilities but UNIX redesigned them all

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Historical (UNIX)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*UNIX V1 utilities
  1971
  SY:2
  # cat, cp, ls, mv, rm, wc, ed, sort, uniq, comm
  # Ken Thompson and Dennis Ritchie

*ed
  1971
  SY:2
  # Ken Thompson, the standard UNIX editor
  # "ed is the standard text editor"

*sed
  1974
  ed
  SY:2
  # Lee McMahon, stream editor derived from ed

*grep
  1973
  ed
  SY:2
  # Ken Thompson, derived from ed's g/re/p command
egrep
  1979
  # Al Aho, extended regex (alternation, +, ?)
fgrep
  1979
  # Al Aho, fixed-string search using Aho-Corasick

cmp
  1971
  # UNIX V1, byte-by-byte file compare

comm
  1971
  # UNIX V1, common lines between sorted files

uniq
  1971
  # UNIX V1, filter consecutive duplicate lines

*diff
  1974
  SY:2
  # Doug McIlroy, based on Hunt-McIlroy LCS algorithm
  # output format became standard for patch

*sort
  1971
  SY:2
  # UNIX V1, external merge sort

*find
  1971
  SY:2
  # UNIX V1, walk directory tree and match predicates

*tar
  1979
  SY:2
  # UNIX V7, tape archiver

*cpio
  1977
  SY:2
  # PWB/UNIX, copy file archives

*file
  1973
  SY:2
  # UNIX V4, identify file types via magic numbers

*dc
  1971
  SY:2
  # Robert Morris, desk calculator, reverse polish
*bc
  1975
  dc
  SY:2
  # Lorinda Cherry, C-like front end to dc

*tr
  1973
  SY:2
  # UNIX V4, transliterate characters

*od
  1971
  SY:2
  # UNIX V1, octal dump

*dd
  1974
  SY:2
  # UNIX V5, convert and copy, JCL-inspired syntax

*patch
  1985
  diff
  SY:2
  # Larry Wall, apply diff output to files

*tee
  1974
  SY:2
  # UNIX V5, copy stdin to stdout and files

*xargs
  1984
  SY:2
  # UNIX System V, build and execute commands from stdin

ps
  1971
  # UNIX V1, process status

date
  1971
  # UNIX V1, print/set date and time

cal
  1971
  # UNIX V1, calendar display

split
  1971
  # UNIX V1, split files into pieces

basename
  1979
  # UNIX V7, extract filename from path

strings
  1979
  # UNIX V7, extract printable strings from binary files

*more
  1978
  # Dan Halbert, BSD, first pager
*less
  1984
  more
  # Mark Nudelman, opposite of more
  # backward scrolling, search, much more capable

*awk
  1978
  SY:2
  # Aho, Weinberger, Kernighan; pattern-action language
*nawk
  1985
  # "new awk", added functions, multiple input streams
  # described in "The AWK Programming Language" (1988)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plan 9
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*Plan 9 utilities
  1993
  SY:1
  # reimplemented from scratch, cleaner than UNIX versions
  # UTF-8 native, simpler flags, /proc-based ps

*sam
  1987
  ed
  SY:1
  # Rob Pike, structural regular expressions
  # influenced acme, Plan 9's primary editor before acme

*acme
  1993
  sam
  SY:1
  # Rob Pike, editor/shell/window system hybrid
  # mouse-driven, plumber integration

*hoc
  1984
  bc
  SY:1
  # Kernighan & Pike, "The UNIX Programming Environment"
  # higher-order calculator, yacc-based

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Open source (GNU)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*GNU sed
  1989
  sed
  SY:2
  # Jay Fenlason, Tom Lord, Ken Pizzini, Paolo Bonzini

*GNU Coreutils
  1992
  SY:2
  # merger of fileutils, textutils, shellutils (2003)
  # Jim Meyering et al, GNU versions of standard UNIX utilities
GNU Coreutils 5.0
  2003
  # first merged release
GNU Coreutils 8.6
  2010
  # sort --parallel (multithreaded sorting)
GNU Coreutils 9.0
  2021
  # copy-on-write cp, AVX2-accelerated wc

*GNU grep
  1992
  grep
  SY:2
  # Mike Haertel, fast implementation using Boyer-Moore
GNU grep 2.5
  2002
  # PCRE support (-P)
GNU grep 3.8
  2022
  # switched from PCRE to PCRE2

*GNU diff
  1988
  diff
  SY:2
  # Paul Eggert, Myers O(ND) algorithm
GNU diffutils 3.4
  2016
  # colorized output (--color)

*gawk
  1986
  nawk
  SY:2
  # Arnold Robbins, GNU awk
  # networking, persistent arrays, namespaces (5.0, 2019)

mawk
  1991
  nawk
  # Mike Brennan, fast bytecode-compiled awk

*GNU tar
  1990
  tar
  SY:2
  # John Gilmore, extended tar format
GNU tar 1.14
  2004
  # PAX/POSIX.1-2001 archive format support
GNU tar 1.22
  2009
  # xz compression support (-J)

*GNU find
  1990
  find
  SY:2
  # part of findutils, with xargs

*gzip
  1993
  SY:2
  # Jean-loup Gailly, Mark Adler
  # DEFLATE algorithm, replaced compress (LZW patent issues)
gzip 1.0
  1993
  # first stable release

*bzip2
  1996
  gzip
  SY:2
  # Julian Seward, Burrows-Wheeler block sorting

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Open source (modern replacements)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*ripgrep
  2016
  GNU grep
  # Andrew Gallant, Rust, very fast recursive grep
  # respects .gitignore, Unicode-aware

*fd
  2017
  GNU find
  # David Peter, Rust, simpler find alternative

*exa
  2014
  # modern ls replacement, colors, git integration
eza
  2023
  exa
  # community fork after exa was abandoned

*bat
  2018
  # cat with syntax highlighting and git integration

*sd
  2018
  # Rust, simpler sed alternative for find-and-replace

*fzf
  2013
  # Junegunn Choi, fuzzy finder for command-line

*jq
  2012
  # Stephen Dolan, JSON processor, like sed/awk for JSON

*xz
  2009
  bzip2
  # Lasse Collin, LZMA2 compression, better ratio than bzip2

*zstd
  2016
  gzip
  SY:6
  # Yann Collet, Facebook, fast compression
  # near-lz4 speed at near-zlib ratio

*pigz
  2007
  gzip
  # Mark Adler, parallel gzip

delta
  2019
  diff
  # syntax-highlighting diff pager, used as git pager

difftastic
  2021
  diff
  # structural/AST-aware diff, understands language syntax

dust
  2018
  # Rust du replacement, visual disk usage

procs
  2019
  # Rust ps replacement, colored output, tree view

choose
  2020
  # Rust, simpler cut/awk field selection

zoxide
  2020
  # smarter cd, learns frequently used directories

broot
  2019
  # interactive tree/file navigator

hyperfine
  2018
  # command-line benchmarking tool, statistical analysis

lsd
  2018
  # ls with colors and icons

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Industry
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*MS-DOS utilities
  1981
  SY:6
  # DIR, COPY, DEL, TYPE, etc.
  # influenced by CP/M

*BusyBox
  1999
  GNU Coreutils
  SY:2
  # Bruce Perens (started 1996), single binary with many utilities
  # standard on embedded Linux, Android

*toybox
  2012
  BusyBox
  # Rob Landley, BSD-licensed BusyBox replacement
  # used in Android since 6.0

*uutils
  2013
  GNU Coreutils
  # Rust rewrite of GNU coreutils, cross-platform

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Editors (related)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*vi
  1976
  ed
  SY:2
  # Bill Joy, visual mode for ex (ex was ed extension)
*nvi
  1994
  # Keith Bostic, BSD vi rewrite
*vim
  1991
  vi
  SY:2
  # Bram Moolenaar
*neovim
  2014
  vim
  # Thiago de Arruda, refactored vim with async plugins, Lua

*Emacs
  1976
  # Guy Steele and Richard Stallman, MIT
*GNU Emacs
  1984
  Emacs
  SY:2
  # Richard Stallman

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Academic
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

McIlroy pipes
  1964
  # Doug McIlroy's memo proposing pipes
  # implemented in UNIX V3 (1973), foundation of utility composition

Thompson NFA
  1968
  # Ken Thompson, CACM, regex compiled to NFA
  # linear-time matching, basis of grep/sed/awk regex engines

*Aho-Corasick
  1975
  grep
  # Aho & Corasick, multi-pattern string matching
  # used by fgrep, later by many search tools

Software Tools
  1976
  # Kernighan & Plauger, book reimplementing UNIX utilities in Ratfor
  # very influential on composable utility design

Boyer-Moore
  1977
  # fast string search by skipping characters
  # the reason GNU grep is so fast

*diff (Myers)
  1986
  diff
  # Eugene Myers, "An O(ND) Difference Algorithm" (Algorithmica)
  # used by GNU diff, git diff, most modern diff implementations

Patience diff
  2005
  diff
  # Bram Cohen, produces more human-readable diffs
  # used by git as diff.algorithm=patience
